items.title: Sectoral Deployment of Bank Credit – March 2026
items.summary: Data on sectoral deployment of bank credit for the month of March 2026, collected from 41 select scheduled commercial banks (SCBs) which together account for about 95 per cent of the total non-food credit by all SCBs 1 , are set out in Statements I and II . On a year-on-year (y-o-y) basis, non-food bank credit 2 grew by 15.9 per cent as on the fortnight ended March 31, 2026, compared to 10.9 per cent during the corresponding fortnight of the previous year (i.e., April 04, 2025). Highlights of the sectoral deployment of bank credit as on the fortnight ended March 31, 2026 are given below: Credi

items.summary: Reserve Bank of India announces the auction of Government of India Treasury Bills as per the following details: Sr. No. Treasury Bill Notified Amount (₹ crore) Auction Date Settlement Date 1 91-Day 12,000 May 06, 2026 (Wednesday) May 07, 2026 (Thursday) 2 182-Day 6,000 3 364-Day 6,000 Total 24,000 The sale will be subject to the terms and conditions specified in the General Notification F.No.4(2)-B(W&M)/2018 dated March 26, 2025 , issued by Government of India, as amended from time to time. State Governments, Union Territories with legislature, eligible Provident Funds in India, designated For

items.summary: The values of exports and imports of services during March 2026 are presented in the following table. International Trade in Services (US$ million) Month Receipts (Exports) Payments (Imports) January – 2026 38,165 (9.8) 16,631 (-0.5) February – 2026 34,708 (9.7) 16,865 (16.2) March – 2026 38,208 (7.2) 17,210 (-1.6) Note: (i) Data for January-March are provisional; and (ii) Figures in parentheses are growth rates over the corresponding month of the previous year, which have been revised on the basis of balance of payments statistics. items.title: Lending and Deposit Rates of Scheduled Commercial Banks – April 2026
items.summary: Data on lending and deposit rates of scheduled commercial banks (SCBs) (excluding regional rural banks and small finance banks) received during the month of April 2026 are set out in Tables 1 to 9. Highlights: Lending Rates: The weighted average lending rate (WALR) on fresh rupee loans of SCBs stood at 8.40 per cent in March 2026 (8.44 per cent in February 2026). The WALR on outstanding rupee loans of SCBs marginally declined to 8.99 per cent in March 2026 from 9.00 per cent in February 2026. 1-Year median Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate (MCLR) of SCBs increased to 8.55 per cent in A

items.summary: The following State Governments have offered to sell stock by way of auction, for an aggregate amount of ₹18,600 Crore (Face Value). items.title: RBI approves the voluntary amalgamation of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) with TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra)
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) with TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra). The Scheme has been sanctioned in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The Scheme will come into force with effect from May 04, 2026 (Monday). The branches of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) will function as branches of TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra) with effect from May 04, 2026. (Brij Raj) Chief General Manage

items.title: Final redemption under Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme - Redemption Price for final redemption of SGB 2018-19 Series-I due on May 04, 2026
items.summary: In terms of GOI notification F.No.4(8)-W&M/2018 dated April 13, 2018 (SGB 2018-19 Series-I-Issue date May 04, 2018) on Sovereign Gold Bond Scheme, the Gold Bond shall be repayable on the expiration of eight years from the date of issue of the Gold Bonds. Accordingly, the final redemption date of the above tranche shall be May 04, 2026. 2. Further, the redemption price of SGB shall be based on simple average of closing price of gold of 999 purity of previous three business days from the date of redemption, as published by the India Bullion and Jewellers Association Ltd (IBJA). Accordingly, the

items.title: 46th Half-Yearly Report on Management of Foreign Exchange Reserves: October 2025 - March 2026
items.summary: It may be recalled that in February 2004 , the Reserve Bank initiated the practice of compiling half-yearly reports on the management of foreign exchange reserves and making them public to promote greater transparency and enhance disclosure in the management of the country’s foreign exchange reserves. In this connection, Reserve Bank of India has today released the 46th Half-Yearly Report on Management of Foreign Exchange Reserves with reference to end-March 2026. During October 2025-March 2026, India’s foreign exchange reserves decreased from USD 700.09 billion to USD 691.11 billion. The late

items.title: Withdrawal of ₹2000 Denomination Banknotes – Status
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) had announced the withdrawal of ₹2000 denomination banknotes from circulation vide Press Release 2023-2024/257 dated May 19, 2023 . The status of withdrawal of ₹2000 banknotes is periodically published by the RBI. The last press release in this regard was published on April 01, 2026 . 2. The facility for deposit and / or exchange of the ₹2000 banknotes was available at all bank branches in the country upto October 07, 2023. 3. The facility for exchange of the ₹2000 banknotes is available at the 19 Issue Offices of the Reserve Bank (RBI Issue Offices) 1 since May

items.title: IRFC expands beyond Indian Railways with ₹13,527-crore refinancing deal for Hyderabad Metro
items.summary: The state-backed financier is leveraging its core strength in transportation finance to support urban mobility and help the Telangana government-owned project replace its high-cost debt.

items.title: SEBI proposes dynamic option strike price framework
items.summary: Exchanges may be required to introduce new option strikes intraday to ensure trading continuity during sharp market moves

items.title: Broker’s Call: Prestige Estates (Buy)
items.summary: Axis Securities

items.title: 'Inflation and unholy mess:' Why Nithin Kamath thinks RBI may not stay soft on interest rates
items.summary: Nithin Kamath warned that a weak monsoon caused by El Niño and rising global oil prices linked to the Iran conflict could sharply increase inflation in India. He said higher food and fuel costs may force the RBI to raise interest rates, hurting growth and market sentiment.

items.title: India proposes broad revamp of industrial output index ahead of new series
items.summary: India's statistics ministry is set to unveil a new way to measure industrial output next week. Key changes include expanding the sectors tracked and adopting a chain-linked framework. The base year will be updated, and new sectors like renewable power and waste management will be included. This aims to provide a more accurate picture of India's evolving industrial landscape.

items.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

items.title: India bars piped natural gas customers from buying LPG cylinders, order shows
items.summary: India has banned customers using piped natural gas from purchasing LPG cylinders. This move comes as LPG supplies to some industries are reduced due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. The government is encouraging the use of piped gas. India relies heavily on LPG imports, with a significant portion coming from the Middle East.

items.title: RVNL Q4 Results: Net profit drops 59% YoY to ₹187 crore; revenue rises 4%, final dividend announced
items.summary: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 4.2% YoY to ₹6,695.9 crore, but net profit fell 59% YoY to ₹187 crore. For FY26, revenue increased slightly, yet net profit declined 32% to ₹875 crore.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: LIC, Max Healthcare among 7 big movers on Monday
items.summary: ​Domestic equity markets extended gains for a third straight session. LIC, Vodafone Idea, Eicher Motors and Titagarh Rail Systems surged, while JSW Cement and Max Healthcare declined.

items.title: Puravankara secures 14.6-acre in Mandur, Bengaluru with Rs 2,300 cr development value
items.summary: Realty developer Puravankara has acquired a significant 14.6-acre land parcel in Mandur, Bengaluru. This strategic acquisition carries a potential gross development value of Rs 2,300 crore. The project will feature a saleable area of approximately 1.8 million sq ft. This move strengthens Puravankara's Bengaluru landbank to 25.61 million sq ft.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: Ajmera Realty sales jump, Crizac hits upper circuit, Sundaram Finance profit up, Salzer Electronics stock down
items.summary: Q4 Results Today Live: Find all the latest update on the financial results of the companies scheduled to announce, here.

items.title: NTPC starts site selection studies in Gujrat, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, Madhya Pradesh for nuclear projects
items.summary: State-owned NTPC is exploring new nuclear power projects. Site selection studies have begun in Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and Odisha. This initiative is part of NTPC's extensive 30 GW nuclear energy plan. The company aims to contribute significantly to India's ambitious nuclear capacity target. Investments will be substantial, spanning multiple states. NTPC is diversifying its energy portfolio.

items.title: Pine Labs Q4 Results: Co turns to black, logs Rs 59 crore profit on 17% revenue jump
items.summary: Pine Labs Q4 Results: Payments solutions provider Pine Labs turned profitable in the March-ended quarter, posting a consolidated net profit of Rs 59 crore versus a net loss of Rs 29 crore in the year ago period.

items.title: Quote of the day by Philip Fisher: "The big profits I have made were through very long planning, waiting and watching"
items.summary: Philip Fisher, a pioneer of growth investing, emphasized that significant profits stem from meticulous long-term planning, patient waiting, and diligent observation. This timeless lesson highlights that true wealth creation in investing is a disciplined, slow process, not a rushed endeavor, urging investors to focus on quality businesses and allow compounding to work over extended periods.

items.title: FM asks lenders to go beyond standard loans, design credit repayments around biz cycles
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has called on banks to move beyond standard loan products. She emphasized the need for repayment structures that align with the specific income cycles of different businesses. This initiative aims to provide tailored credit solutions for MSMEs, supporting their growth and contributing to a stronger middle class and a developed India.

items.title: Vegorama Punjabi Angithi IPO allotment expected today: Here's how to check status
items.summary: The Vegorama Punjabi Angithi IPO allotment is expected today, May 25, after receiving nearly 35 times overall subscription. Retail investors will be allotted shares on a proportional basis, with refunds starting May 26. The IPO price was fixed at ₹77 per share, with a listing date of May 27.

items.title: Oil companies' losses down to Rs 600 cr per day following fuel price hikes
items.summary: State-owned oil companies have seen their daily losses reduce to under Rs 600 crore. This comes after four rounds of petrol and diesel price increases. Previously, losses stood at about Rs 1,000 crore daily. These figures include losses from domestic LPG sales. Fuel prices were frozen despite rising crude oil costs.

items.title: After 4 rounds of fuel price hike, oil marketing companies ‘losing around Rs 600 cr per day: Ministry of (MoPNG), Sujata Sharma
Sebi proposes intraday option strike additions to help traders navigate volatility
items.summary: The market regulator aims to standardize strike management across exchanges, ensuring that traders aren't left without relevant contracts when prices swing sharply during the day.

items.title: Kalpataru inks Rs 1,250 crore cluster redevelopment in Mumbai’s Kandivali
items.summary: Realty developer Kalpataru has secured a significant cluster redevelopment project in Ashok Nagar, Kandivali. This project, spanning 2.8 acres, involves five housing societies and has a gross development value of approximately Rs 1,250 crore. The development will feature residential spaces and high-street retail.

items.title: LIC hikes stake in Central Bank of India to 6.06%
Bengaluru airport operator BIAL hands over Rs 16 crore dividend to Karnataka govt arm
items.summary: Bengaluru International Airport Limited, or BIAL, has given a dividend of over Rs 16 crore to the Karnataka State Industrial and Infrastructure Development Corporation. This dividend is for the financial year 2025-26. KSIIDC holds a 13% stake in BIAL. This marks the first dividend from BIAL since 2017-18. Profits were previously reinvested for airport expansion.

items.title: Infra projects see cost overrun of Rs 5.65 lakh crore in April
items.summary: Central sector infrastructure projects exceeding Rs 150 crore each show a cumulative cost overrun of Rs 5.65 lakh crore. As of April, 1,981 projects are monitored with a revised cost of Rs 42.78 lakh crore. Expenditure stands at Rs 20.36 lakh crore, indicating steady implementation. Transport and logistics lead with the highest number of projects and revised costs.

items.title: Nifty crosses 24,000 on US-Iran peace hopes, crude plunge
items.summary: The Nifty 50 closed at 24,031.70, up 312.40 points (1.32%), marking its first close above 24,000 in recent sessions; The BSE Sensex ended at 76,488.96, gaining 1,073.61 points (1.42%)

items.title: Air India working on elevating passenger experience with new lounge at San Francisco airport
items.summary: Air India is enhancing passenger experiences by opening its Maharaja Lounge in San Francisco. This marks the first lounge opening outside India since the Tata Group's takeover. The airline is transforming into a world-class carrier with an Indian heart. This expansion reflects a commitment to North America, a key market.

items.title: RBI to examine role of quantum technology in strengthening financial sector
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is forming an expert committee to study quantum technology's role in finance. This new technology could revolutionize financial tasks but also poses security risks. The committee will assess current systems and recommend ways to make India's financial sector quantum-safe. The report is expected within six months.

items.title: Sebi proposes dynamic options strike framework to tackle volatility, improve trading continuity
items.summary: Securities and Exchange Board of India has proposed a new framework for managing options strike prices during volatile trading sessions. The plan aims to improve strike availability, enable intraday additions without system disruptions, and give exchanges flexibility across equity, currency and commodity derivatives markets.

items.title: India's wheat procurement surges past 34 million tonnes, bolsters food buffers
items.summary: Punjab led the procurement process, with around 12.16 mt of wheat purchased by government agencies, followed by Madhya Pradesh with 10.14 mt and Haryana 8.12 mt. Among the other wheat-producing states, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh purchased around 2.1 mt and 1.55 mt respectively until 24 May.

items.title: Rupee gains for third straight session, settles at 95.25 against dollar
items.summary: The rupee gained as Brent crude prices fell sharply and the dollar index weakened

items.title: Zepto planning to file papers for $1 billion IPO in June: Report
items.summary: Zepto is preparing for a potential $1 billion IPO, with a public filing expected in June and launch targeted for July. The quick-commerce firm plans to use proceeds for expansion as India’s IPO market seeks renewed momentum amid slowing deal activity and global economic concerns.

items.title: After Mumbai High, ONGC brings in BP in other western offshore oil, gas fields
items.summary: In a strategic alliance, ONGC has teamed up with BP to enhance the extraction of oil and gas from its aging offshore fields in the west. This partnership is aimed at revitalising production levels, drawing from the encouraging outcomes observed in their earlier collaboration at Mumbai High.

items.title: VT Markets expands to 500+ US stocks with 39 new AI, Space and Energy picks
items.summary: The additions reflect a broader market shift from consumer-facing AI narratives toward the underlying infrastructure driving AI adoption — including optical networking, electricity generation, and data centre equipment

items.title: Ashok Leyland secures order for 715 vehicles from VRL Logistics
items.summary: Ashok Leyland has received a significant order for 715 vehicles from VRL Logistics. The order includes haulage trucks and staff buses. Deliveries are underway, with 300 trucks already supplied. The remaining vehicles will be delivered this year. This strengthens the partnership between the two companies. The new vehicles feature advanced technology for better productivity and profitability.

items.title: Govt gives up Rs 14,000 crore in tax revenue after excise duty cut on petrol and diesel: Petroleum Ministry
items.summary: The Indian government has sacrificed nearly Rs 14,000 crore in tax revenue by lowering excise duty on petrol and diesel. This move aims to protect consumers from rising fuel prices driven by the West Asia crisis. Despite this, oil companies continue to face daily losses.

items.title: The 90% vulnerability: Inside Ajoy Chawla’s masterplan to rewire Titan
items.summary: A stellar quarter and ₹76,000 crore in annual revenue couldn’t save Titan from a brutal market crash. As gold tariffs rise and macro shocks hit Tanishq, can the retail giant successfully pivot to a diversified lifestyle future?

items.title: Ajmera Realty share price zooms 10% after Q4 results 2026
items.summary: Ajmera Realty & Infra India reported a 9.6% gain on May 25 after strong FY26 financial results, with revenue surging 46% to ₹1,098 crore. Collections and net profit also rose significantly, leading to an ambitious FY27 pre-sales target of ₹2,200 crore.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: Masterclass on new era of wealth creation, allocation and preservation
items.summary: The ET Alpha Summit in Mumbai will convene leading investors to navigate a changing market landscape. Experts will offer high-conviction insights on investment strategies, wealth preservation, and succession planning. Attendees will gain actionable ideas to make crucial decisions in today's evolving financial environment.

items.title: Zepto said to plan June public filing for $1 billion India IPO
items.summary: Deliberations are ongoing and details, including the size and timing of the IPO may still change

items.title: India medical tourism market feels the weight of Iran war
items.summary: India's medical tourism sector faces challenges as West Asian tensions continue. Overseas patient numbers remain lower than before the conflict intensified. Hospital chains report a gradual increase in inquiries and arrivals, but traffic has not fully recovered. Prolonged geopolitical uncertainty could also raise operating costs through inflation and currency fluctuations.

items.title: JM Financial launches second ₹1,000 crore credit AIF
items.summary: The fund aims to provide structured credit solutions to businesses while delivering risk-adjusted returns to investors

items.title: Adequate availability of fertilisers for upcoming Kharif sowing season: Govt
items.summary: India is set for a robust Kharif season with ample fertilizer supplies. Domestic production and imports have ensured comfortable stock levels. Over 200 lakh tonne of fertilizers are currently available, exceeding 50 percent of the projected demand. This proactive measure guarantees sufficient soil nutrient availability for farmers as sowing begins in June.

items.title: Harikanta Overseas IPO Day 4: Issue booked over 50% so far. Check GMP and other details about this SME offer
items.summary: Harikanta Overseas IPO worth ₹24.30 crore is entirely a fresh issue of 0.27 crore shares, priced in the range of ₹86 to ₹91 apiece. Since the SME IPO has no offer-for-sale (OFS) component, all proceeds will be received by the company.

items.title: Beware the boom and bust cycle of memory stocks, investors warn amid AI excitement
items.summary: "In the long run, it's a pretty dreadful industry," William de Gale of BlueBox Asset Management, told CNBC's Europe Early Edition on Wednesday.

items.title: Oil cos keep bleeding, on slippery slope with Rs 600-cr loss per day despite multiple petrol, diesel price hikes
items.summary: Petrol, diesel price hike: Despite the latest round of increases, officials acknowledged that current retail prices are still insufficient to fully offset the sharp rise in international crude costs, leaving public sector fuel retailers exposed to substantial daily losses.

items.title: Crude oil prices tumble over 4% on hopes of US-Iran breakthrough
items.summary: Reports suggesting progress toward reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil shipping route, eased fears of supply disruptions and triggered a sharp selloff in oil futures

items.title: Hyderabad Metro to turn profitable next year with IRFC’s low-cost loan: Telangana Chief Secretary
items.summary: Hyderabad Metro is set to become profitable next year. Indian Railways Finance Corporation has provided a Rs 13,527 crore loan to refinance existing debt. This move is expected to reduce financing costs by 30-40%. Telangana state is also acquiring the metro project from L&T.

items.title: Two years on, Railways yet to offer medical aid to parcel staff despite labour commissioner's plea
items.summary: Private workers loading and unloading railway parcels have waited two and a half years for medical facilities. The Chief Labour Commissioner requested the Railway Board to provide these essential services. Workers often sustain injuries but must pay for their own treatment. Despite appeals, the Railway Board has not responded.

items.title: ​Valuation Watch: 11 Largecap stocks with sky-high PE ratios
items.summary: ​NSE largecap stocks with TTM P/E ratios above 70 reflect strong investor growth expectations, but elevated valuations may also indicate overpricing risks, requiring careful assessment of company fundamentals and market conditions.

items.title: Shriram Properties FY26 net profit up 30%, revenue at record high
items.summary: Shriram Properties achieved its highest annual profit and revenue in fiscal year 2025-26. The company's net profit rose 30% to Rs 100.8 crore, with total revenue up 39% to Rs 1,356.9 crore. Strong home deliveries and collections fueled this growth. The developer also expanded into Pune and resolved land matters in Kolkata, showing robust business performance and future potential.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex jumps over 1,000 points as easing crude prices lift Nifty above 24,000
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE :Indian benchmark indices surged on Monday, with the Sensex soaring over 1,000 points and the Nifty crossing 24,000, driven by easing crude oil prices, improving global sentiment, strong financial stocks and optimism surrounding a potential US-Iran deal.

items.title: Oil slump, central bank support manifest in rupee’s three-day winning streak
items.summary: The Indian rupee saw its longest winning streak in a month, closing stronger against the dollar. This rise was driven by the Reserve Bank of India's market interventions and a significant drop in crude oil prices. Hopes for a U.S.-Iran peace deal boosted investor confidence.

items.title: Aluminium futures rise on firm global cues
items.summary: Globally, Aluminium futures on the London Metal Exchange (LME) went up by $12.85, or 0.35 per cent, to $3,650.90 per tonne

items.title: Italian bank CDP to raise stake in payments group Nexi after CVC weighs bid
items.summary: Cassa Depositi e Prestiti’s move comes as buyout firm had considered €9bn bid for Milan-listed group

items.title: Suzlon Energy Q4 results 2026: Consolidated profit falls 6% YoY to ₹1,114 crore
items.summary: Suzlon Energy Q4 results 2026: Consolidated profit declined 6% YoY to ₹1,114 crore, while revenue from operations for the March quarter jumped 45% YoY to ₹5,468.06 crore. EBITDA at ₹964 crore jumped 39% YoY.

items.title: Gold futures rise to ₹1.59 lakh/10g on weak dollar, US-Iran peace deal hopes
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, the yellow metal for the June delivery increased by ₹426, or 0.27%, to ₹1,59,105 per 10 grams in a business turnover of 5,312 lots

items.title: Nifty Metal's 18% YTD rally adds ₹1.6 lakh crore to investor wealth. Time to be euphoric or cautious?
items.summary: Nifty Metal is the leading sectoral performer on NSE following a 18% rally, adding ₹1,60,503 crore to investor wealth. At the same time, the Nifty 50 index has slumped by over 8%.

items.title: abcoffee raises ₹61 cr in pre-series-B round led by Kliff Ventures
items.summary: The fresh capital will be used to accelerate abcoffee's disciplined, cluster-led expansion across existing and new markets, as per the statement

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 25: HFCL, Adani Power, Eicher Motors, OLA, Tata Motors, HPCL among top gainers
items.summary: Indian markets ended May 25 with strong gains, as Nifty 50 rose 1.4% and BSE Sensex climbed 1.42%, driven by falling oil prices and optimism for a US-Iran peace deal. Broader indices also showed positive trends amidst improved risk sentiment.

items.title: Puravankara acquires 15 acre land in Bengaluru for housing project; eyes Rs 2,300 crore revenue
items.summary: Puravankara Ltd has acquired a significant 14.57-acre land parcel in Bengaluru. This acquisition is set to pave the way for a new housing project with an impressive revenue potential of around Rs 2,300 crore. The company's strategic move strengthens its presence in the Bengaluru real estate market. This development underscores Puravankara's commitment to expanding its project pipeline.

items.title: Global Markets | European shares climb to over two-month highs on Iran-US peace optimism
items.summary: The pan-European STOXX 600 was up 0.61% at 628.93 points, as ​of 0712 GMT, trading just shy of a record high touched in late February, ​just before the Middle East war erupted.

items.title: Yaashvi Jewellers IPO Day 1: Issue sees tepid demand, booked only 2% so far. Check price band, offer size, key details
items.summary: Yaashvi Jewellers IPO runs from May 25 to May 27, priced at ₹83 per share. The company aims to raise ₹44 crore for working capital and debt repayment. Currently, the IPO subscription status is 2%, with the retail portion at 4% and NII at 1%.

items.title: PSU closure: Govt sets in motion new move after years wasted in stop-start process
items.summary: The Centre is pushing ministries to accelerate the closure of unprofitable state-owned companies. Progress has been slow despite a policy approved eight years ago. Only one company has effectively ceased operations. The government aims to expedite these closures, addressing concerns over delays in both disinvestment and shutdown processes.

items.title: West Asia conflict may dent India Inc profitability by 200 bps: Crisil
items.summary: A prolonged West Asia conflict poses a risk to India Inc's profitability. Crisil Ratings predicts a significant drop in operating margins due to supply chain issues and rising costs. However, strong company balance sheets are expected to protect overall credit quality. Some sectors like ceramics and airlines face severe stress. Other export-oriented sectors might benefit from a weaker rupee.

items.title: Hindalco investors bank on silver linings beyond March quarter, but valuations are demanding
items.summary: Hindalco's Q4FY26 net profit halved due to exceptional charges, but surging aluminium and copper prices, Novelis' Oswego plant restart, and upward earnings revisions by brokerages keep the medium-term outlook intact.

items.title: Suzlon Energy Q4 Results: Profit falls 6% YoY to Rs 1,114 crore despite 45% revenue surge
items.summary: Suzlon Energy Q4 Results: Revenue from operations, however, increased 45% YoY to Rs 5,468 crore.

items.title: Panattoni plans 1.52 lakh sq ft manufacturing facility in Hyderabad
items.summary: Industrial real estate developer Panattoni is entering Hyderabad with a Rs 150 crore advanced manufacturing project. This build-to-own facility will span 10 acres and focus on aerospace and high-precision manufacturing. Construction begins in July and will take 15 months. The project aims to support around 500 jobs in the region. Panattoni's India operations started in 2022.

items.title: Vinay Rajani sees Nifty extending rally toward 24,400; recommends PFC and L&T Finance
items.summary: Nifty is set to climb towards 24,400. Financial and rate-sensitive sectors are leading the market. Broader market indices are also showing strong uptrends. Analysts see this as a healthy sign of widespread buying. Traders can use dips to 23,800 as a buying opportunity. Two stock picks, Power Finance Corporation and L&T Finance, are recommended with defined risk.

items.title: India will be the largest beneficiary of cooling global oil prices: Sunil Shah
items.summary: The fall in oil prices came amid reports that Iran had agreed in principle to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and dispose of its stockpile of highly enriched uranium under a developing agreement with the US

items.title: 5 listed Indian REITs distribute over Rs 2,566 crore to unitholders in Q4FY26
items.summary: Five listed Indian REITs distributed over Rs 2,566 crore to more than 4.25 lakh unitholders in Q4 FY26, with full-year distributions exceeding Rs 8,900 crore. This marks a significant milestone for the Indian REIT industry, showcasing strong growth in distributions and increasing investor confidence in the sector.

items.title: Nifty 50 breaks above 24,000? Here are the next key levels to watch
items.summary: According to market experts, the 24,000 level remains a critical hurdle for the Nifty 50 as it coincides with the 52-day exponential moving average (DEMA). Sustaining above this zone will be essential for the continuation of upward momentum.

items.title: Schroders Capital plans to build on $1 bn India investment as scope for early investor exits grows beyond IPOs
items.summary: The company is scouting for opportunities in sectors including consumer, healthcare, technology and artificial intelligence.

items.title: Commodity Radar: Gold's bullish trend intact despite current consolidation. Buy for these targets
items.summary: MCX gold prices continue to consolidate near record levels, supported by safe-haven demand amid global uncertainty, with analysts maintaining a positive bias and suggesting a buy-on-dips strategy. Technical indicators point to sustained bullish momentum, though consolidation may continue until a decisive breakout above key resistance levels emerges.

items.title: India’s listed REITs distribute over Rs 8,900 crore in FY26
items.summary: The five publicly listed REITs, Brookfield India Real Estate Trust, Embassy Office Parks REIT, Knowledge Realty Trust, Mindspace Business Parks REIT and Nexus Select Trust, together distributed over Rs 8,900 crore during FY26, marking a year-on-year growth of more than 50%.

items.title: Big push for Hyderabad Metro! IRFC signs ₹13,527 crore refinancing deal
items.summary: IRFC shares rose 4% to ₹102 after securing a ₹13,527 crore loan from L&TMRHL to refinance Hyderabad Metro Rail project debts. The refinancing enhances financial sustainability and supports the expansion of Hyderabad’s metro network, which currently serves over 500,000 passengers daily.

items.title: India's power transmission infra set for Rs 9 trillion upgrade as renewable push accelerates: Report
items.summary: India is set for a massive power transmission investment of nearly Rs 9 trillion by 2032, according to a Kotak Neo report. This will support the country's clean energy goals by building essential grid infrastructure. Renewable energy capacity is growing rapidly, requiring extensive transmission lines and substations. High-Voltage Direct Current systems will be crucial.

items.title: SIP boom a bane for Indian stock market: Is retail money giving FPIs a smooth exit and hurting rupee?
items.summary: There is a debate that a surge in retail investments through SIPs has provided foreign portfolio investors a smooth exit from Indian equities, leading to significant currency pressures.

items.title: REITs distribute over ₹8,900 crore to unitholders in FY26
items.summary: Q4 payouts by five listed trusts cross ₹2,566 crore; sector market cap stands above ₹1.7 lakh crore

items.title: Prize draw businesses have better luck than the average punter
items.summary: Lack of regulation has helped draw operators to thrive

items.title: Private banks likely to outperform govt banks in earnings growth over next 2 years: Report
items.summary: Private banks are set for stronger earnings growth in the coming two years. Loan growth is expected to be similar for both private and public sector banks. However, private lenders will likely see better profits due to stable margins and fewer risks. Public sector banks may face profitability pressures from various factors. Market valuations are considering current banking sector challenges.

items.title: Oyo parent PRISM's G6 Hospitality launches 38 new hotels across the US
items.summary: PRISM, the company behind Oyo, has launched 38 Studio 6 extended stay hotels across the United States. This expansion follows PRISM's acquisition of G6 Hospitality. The new hotels offer amenities for longer stays, targeting corporate professionals and relocating families. PRISM plans to integrate India-built technology into these properties, aiming for operational efficiency and an elevated guest experience.

items.title: FIIs, retail investors raise stakes in 10 midcaps, but stocks slip up to 30%
items.summary: Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) and retail investors increased their stakes in nearly 20 midcap stocks over the March quarter. Despite this accumulation, 10 of the top laggards declined by 10% to 30% in the last six months, reflecting weak market sentiment.

items.title: Shriram General Insurance ties up with Piramal Finance for product distribution
items.summary: Shriram General Insurance has joined hands with Piramal Finance. This collaboration will bring insurance products to more people. Piramal Finance's large network will help SGI reach customers. The partnership focuses on semi-urban and rural markets. It aims to make insurance easily available. This will help protect customer assets and financial futures.

items.title: Penny stock under ₹10 Shah Metacorp jumps after declaration of ₹49.8 crore rights issue
items.summary: Shah Metacorp share price rose over 6% after announcing a rights issue to raise ₹49.80 crore for expansion. The issue opens on 11 June, offering shares at ₹4.86 each to enhance shareholder stakes. The company also plans to acquire a 26% interest in renewable energy.

items.title: India’s AI boom could turn data centres into the next big infrastructure race: KPMG
items.summary: India's data centre sector is set for massive growth, reaching nearly $46 billion by 2033. AI workloads and cloud adoption are key drivers. The market is shifting towards AI-optimised infrastructure. Challenges lie in execution complexity, with a call for integrated lifecycle partners. Data localisation laws and ESG priorities are also boosting demand for local data centres.

items.title: Markets may have already discounted short-term pain, recovery seen ahead: Dinshaw Irani
items.summary: Indian markets are in a good spot with constructive investor sentiment. Elevated crude oil prices are a concern, but a resolution could boost macro stability. Corporate earnings are showing resilience, especially in mid and smallcaps. While the current quarter may see margin pressure, growth is expected to recover.

items.title: India turns to Latin American, African oil after Hormuz disruption
items.summary: Indian refiners sought oil from Latin America and Africa as Middle East supplies faced disruptions. Imports from Venezuela, Brazil, Angola, and Nigeria increased. Russia remained a key supplier, though its share saw a dip. The UAE and Saudi Arabia saw increased imports. Overall oil imports in April remained steady but decreased year-on-year. Brazil and Venezuela emerged as significant suppliers.

items.title: NBCC Q4 Results: Navratna PSU posts 39% YoY profit growth to ₹256 crore; dividend of ₹0.46 announced
items.summary: NBCC's Q4FY26 profit came in at ₹253.51 crore compared with ₹182.66 crore in the same period last year, recording a growth of 38.78%.

items.title: Park Medi World to acquire Medicity Hospital in Rudrapur, enter Uttarakhand
items.summary: Hospital chain Park Medi World Limited is acquiring The Medicity Hospital in Rudrapur. The deal is valued at Rs 177 crore and is an all-cash transaction. This acquisition marks Park Group's entry into Uttarakhand. The Medicity Hospital is a multi-super speciality facility with 330 beds. This move aligns with the company's growth strategy to expand its network in underserved markets.

items.title: SEBI bans 7 individuals in ₹20-cr stock manipulation case
items.summary: SEBI has also directed finfluencer Hemant Gupta and his sons, Rohan Gupta and Aniket Gupta, to immediately cease and desist from offering unregistered research analyst service

items.title: Singapore reports lower-than-expected inflation for April at 1.8%, revises economic growth higher
items.summary: Core inflation — which strips out prices of private transport and accommodation — came in at 1.4%, against estimates of 1.7%.

items.title: US stock market holidays: Are S&P 500, Nasdaq closed for trading today due to Memorial Day?
items.summary: Memorial Day is observed on the last Monday of May each year to honour and remember military personnel who died while serving the country, both in war and in peacetime.

items.title: HFCL shares soar 10% to fresh record high, skyrocket 172% in four months. Should you buy or wait?
items.summary: HFCL has transitioned from a base breakout to a momentum extension after a sharp rally, says Harshal Dasani of INVasset PMS. The stock has broken past its earlier 52-week high zone, signalling strong momentum, but analysts caution the easy phase of the breakout may be over. The next pullback will help indicate whether buying is institutional or a short-term squeeze.

items.title: Kalpataru expects Rs 1,250 crore revenue from new cluster redevelopment project in Mumbai
items.summary: Realty firm Kalpataru Ltd anticipates a revenue of Rs 1,250 crore from a significant cluster redevelopment project in Ashok Nagar, Kandivali East, Mumbai. This project involves five adjacent societies and spans 2.8 acres. The company views redevelopment as a way to revitalise communities. Kalpataru Ltd is a major real estate developer with numerous completed and ongoing projects across India.

items.title: The three Fs that Sitharaman flagged as India braces for a widening global oil shock, forex strain
items.summary: In light of increasing global unrest from the West Asia crisis and fluctuations in the energy sector, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman is advocating for a concentrated approach to managing fuel, fertiliser, and foreign exchange resources. Prime Minister Modi's recent appeals to conserve foreign currency and reduce discretionary imports underscore this strategy.

items.title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 tops 65,000 for first time as oil falls on Hormuz reopening hopes
items.summary: Japan’s Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time Monday as a sharp decline in oil prices lifted risk sentiment in holiday-thinned trading.

items.title: India turns net importer of finished steel in April, data shows
items.summary: India has become a net importer of finished steel. In April, imports rose significantly while exports also saw an increase. Major suppliers to India included China, South Korea, Japan, Vietnam and Russia. Domestic consumption of finished steel grew, and crude steel production also climbed.

items.title: Stock market holiday this week: BSE and NSE to remain closed for trading on this day
items.summary: This week will be a holiday shortened as BSE and NSE will remain shut for one day. Both the major bourses have declared a stock market holiday this week on Thursday, 28 May 2026, for Bakri Id.

items.title: OMC stocks rally 5.8% as crude slips below $100; fuel prices raised for fourth time in two weeks
items.summary: HPCL led gains with a 5.8% rise to ₹412.55 on the NSE, BPCL advanced over 4% and IOC was up 3.7%

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests L&T Finance, Sandur Manganese shares to buy in near-term
items.summary: Indian equities saw broad-based buying on Monday, with benchmark indices rising over 1%. The Sensex gained nearly 1,000 points, while Nifty 50 approached its 50-day moving average. Positive global cues and improved sentiment boosted investor wealth by nearly ₹5 lakh crore.

items.title: Titagarh, Jupiter Wagons shares rally up to 10% amid reports of Rs 40,000 crore order from Indian Railways
items.summary: Railway stocks surged up to 10% on Monday. Indian Railways is preparing a large tender for one lakh freight wagons. This procurement is expected over the next three to four years. The tender could be worth Rs 40,000 crore. This news boosts companies like Jupiter Wagons, Titagarh Rail Systems, and Texmaco Rail & Engineering. Investors are watching these developments closely.

items.title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman reveals petrol & diesel excise duty cut will hit govt revenues by ₹1 lakh crore
items.summary: Nirmala Sitharaman has confirmed that cutting excise duties on petrol and diesel will result in a revenue loss of ₹1 lakh crore. She also pushed back against a 'pessimistic narrative' on India's economy

items.title: Private banks favoured over PSUs as macro headwinds cloud near-term outlook: Digant Haria
items.summary: Market expert Digant Haria notes strong corporate earnings but rising macroeconomic uncertainty. Private sector banks appear better positioned than PSU banks due to solid fundamentals, though near-term upside may be capped by inflation and currency depreciation. Long-term investors are advised to accumulate, with potential for new highs by Diwali if geopolitical tensions ease.

items.title: JSW Cement shares fall 2% after jumping 14% in two days. Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.summary: JSW Cement shares declined after a sharp post-results rally despite the company reporting a strong jump in March quarter profit and steady revenue growth. Brokerages remained optimistic on the company’s long-term growth prospects, supported by expansion plans, cost-saving initiatives and robust volume guidance, although near-term demand softness and cost pressures remain concerns.

items.title: AI wealth must benefit the public, South Korea's deputy PM says amid Samsung labor tensions
items.summary: South Korea's deputy PM tells CNBC there are concerns as to whether AI could worsen wealth gaps or lead to job losses.

items.title: Ola Electric set for sharp Q1 rebound as registrations cross Q4 levels
items.summary: Ola Electric is witnessing a strong sales recovery, with current-quarter registrations already surpassing the previous quarter's total. The EV maker expects quarterly volumes to nearly double as demand rebounds, supported by a focus on disciplined growth, operational efficiency, and market expansion.

items.title: Delivery Hero shares rise 10% as rival Uber mulls another takeover bid
items.summary: Delivery Hero confirmed in a statement on Saturday that it had received a takeover offer from Uber of €33 per share.

items.title: India to strengthen EV shield against oil imports with fresh subsidies: Report
items.summary: The government is set to significantly boost subsidies for electric two-wheelers. This move under the PM E-Drive scheme aims to promote clean mobility and reduce oil import dependence. The heavy industries ministry is seeking additional funds for this initiative. The scheme has already subsidized a large number of electric two-wheelers.

items.title: India’s kitchens turn into mini gyms as protein orders soar 150%
items.summary: India's embrace of high-protein diets is transforming grocery habits, with quick commerce platforms seeing a 150% surge in protein-related orders over two years. This trend is rapidly expanding beyond metros, with smaller cities showing over 200% faster growth in protein consumption, indicating a shift towards 'better-for-you' eating.

items.title: Nifty holds near 24,000, Sensex above 76,200 at midday; Eicher Motors, Wipro lead gains
items.summary: Eicher Motors remained the top gainer on the Nifty50, trading at ₹7,356, up 5.36 per cent on volumes of over 8.97 lakh shares

items.title: BSE, NSE Holidays 2026: Is the stock market open or closed on May 28?
items.summary: Indian stock markets face a shortened trading week. The National Stock Exchange and BSE will be closed on Thursday, May 28, for Bakri Eid. This is one of 16 stock market holidays planned for 2026. Trading will resume on Friday. The Multi Commodity Exchange will have a partial closure.

items.title: Iran is in the 'process of blinking' over the Strait of Hormuz, Petraeus says
items.summary: Ex-CIA director David Petraeus said that an initial successful peace deal with Tehran would see the Strait opened without any conditions.

items.title: The Great Consumption Reset: How a billion Indians changed what they buy
items.summary: India's consumption landscape is evolving, with consumers favouring experiences, digital services, and premium products over basic goods. Kotak Mutual Fund's report shows a surge in foreign travel spending and premium smartphone sales, indicating a shift towards higher-value purchases.

items.title: Hindalco sees 5% rise in raw material costs, stays confident on key customers
items.summary: Hindalco anticipates a 5% rise in raw material costs. The company is confident in keeping clients like Ford Motor Company. Subsidiary Novelis expects 2027 to be a key year for growth in aluminium can sheet production. Despite disruptions at Novelis' Oswego plant, Hindalco's India business achieved record profits.

items.title: Melody effect: Wrong 'Parle' stock hits 5% upper circuit for 4th day, up 21% since PM Modi's gift for Meloni
items.summary: Parle Industries shares hit the 5% upper circuit for the fourth consecutive session amid speculation that investors are mistaking it for unlisted FMCG major Parle Products. The rally comes as social media discussions around PM Narendra Modi’s ‘Melody’ gift to Italy’s Giorgia Meloni fuel renewed attention on the Parle brand name.

items.title: German firm B+H Solutions to invest 1 mn euro in India to expand nano-fertiliser business
items.summary: German agritech firm B+H Solutions GmbH plans to invest €1 million in India in 2026 to expand its footprint in the country's growing agricultural sector. The company is strengthening its presence with its range of metal-based nano-fertilisers, driven by rising demand, favourable regulatory approvals, and increasing acceptance of nanotechnology among Indian farmers. Designed to improve crop yields, nutrient efficiency, and plant health, B+H Solutions' innovative products are gaining traction as India embraces advanced farming solutions. The investment underscores the growing potential of the In

items.title: Mid & small-cap stocks firm despite oil shocks and global uncertainty: But will the trend continue?
items.summary: Amidst the uncertainties, Indian mid- and small-cap indices have delivered a standout performance in the last two months. The Nifty Smallcap 100 has surged over 20% from its March 2026 lows. The Nifty Midcap 100 has climbed nearly 15%, touching fresh highs in May 2026.

items.title: Huawei plans new smartphone chips this fall as rivalry with Nvidia and Apple heats up
items.summary: Chinese telecom giant Huawei announced Monday a new way of designing chips that improves their capabilities, despite U.S. restrictions on the company.

items.title: FTSE index rejig: Tata Capital, Lenskart, Groww, among six stocks to be included in global indices
items.summary: FTSE index rejig: The companies slated for inclusion are Tata Capital, Lenskart Solutions, LG Electronics India, Meesho, ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company, and Billionbrains Garage Ventures (Groww), Reuters reported.

items.title: Any relief rally in markets likely limited and temporary: David Roche
items.summary: Global markets are reacting prematurely to geopolitical shifts, according to macro strategist David Roche. He argues that recent oil and equity market moves reflect speculation rather than a confirmed resolution, with any potential deal being structurally weak and unlikely to address core strategic issues.

items.title: Roseate Hotels targets asset-light luxury model with managed expansion push
items.summary: Roseate Hotels is shifting to an asset-light strategy and expanding into pilgrimage and lifestyle businesses as competition intensifies in India’s luxury hospitality sector.

items.title: Bitcoin steadies around $77,000 after volatile three-week correction; easing US-Iran tensions lift sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near the $77,000 mark after recovering from a volatile correction phase as easing U.S.-Iran tensions improved investor sentiment. While Ethereum and select altcoins remained under pressure, broader crypto market sentiment stabilised amid hopes of geopolitical de-escalation, although analysts continued to warn about leverage-driven volatility and short-term resistance levels.

items.title: Vodafone Idea, Davangere Sugar, JP Power, HFCL among most traded stocks on NSE today, 25 May 2026
items.summary: Most traded stocks today: Vodafone Idea, Davangere Sugar Company, Jaiprakash Power Ventures (JP Power), HFCL, Fineotex Chemical, and Apollo Micro Systems were the most traded stocks on the NSE.

items.title: Oil market at 'tank bottoms' in Asia, and Europe isn't far behind, warns market veteran Jeff Currie
items.summary: Oil markets are nearing minimum operating levels in Asia, with Europe likely next and the U.S. potentially facing shortages by July, said Carlyle's Jeff Currie.

items.title: RBI dividend provides fiscal buffer, but inflation risks loom large: Kanika Pasricha, Union Bank
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's dividend payout provides a small fiscal buffer. However, government finances face pressure from fuel excise cuts and rising fertiliser subsidies. Inflation risks are increasing due to higher fuel prices. This situation suggests a cautious approach to monetary policy as the central bank monitors economic indicators.

items.title: Aluminium prices near record high: What’s driving the rally?
items.summary: Aluminium prices are trading near multi-year highs due to persistent structural supply tightness, not temporary factors. Stricter environmental rules, high energy costs, and production caps are limiting output, while low global inventories further tighten supply and support elevated prices.

items.title: JM Financial AMC launches second Rs 1,000 crore performing credit AIF to tap private credit opportunity
items.summary: JM Financial Asset Management has launched its second performing credit Alternative Investment Fund (AIF) to tap into the fast-growing private credit market in India and rising demand for structured credit investments. The fund will focus on lending to financially stable companies that are regularly servicing their debt obligations.

items.title: Five value stocks betting big on India’s next growth cycle
items.summary: These five companies combine reasonable valuations with aggressive expansion plans across autos, energy, recycling and solar manufacturing, making them compelling long-term value plays.

items.title: Oracle, Wipro, Tech Mahindra stocks fuel the Nifty IT index above 29,000
items.summary: The Nifty IT index gained almost 1% to rise to the day's high of ₹29,177.80 apiece, with all index constituents in the green. The IT pack rose 4.31% last week, though it remains flat with a negative bias for the month so far.

items.title: 80% return in six months! Healthcare stock Park Medi World hits record high after this acquisition update
items.summary: Park Medi World share price has gained 20% in one month and has jumped 48% in three months. The stock has surged 86% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis.

items.title: Oil marketing companies rally; HPCL shares climb nearly 6%
items.summary: Petrol and diesel prices were raised by ₹2.61-2.71 per litre on Monday, marking the fourth increase in less than two weeks

items.title: 'I don't think I'll ever be able to retire'
items.summary: People in their 70s say they cannot afford to quit their jobs, but others say work keeps them young.

items.title: Rupee gains as crude oil prices fall below $100
items.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said the Indian Rupee is not overvalued and may, in fact, be undervalued after the recent depreciation

items.title: Should you buy Central Bank of India shares as OFS opens today for retail investors?
items.summary: Central Bank of India OFS for retail investors opens on May 25, after a strong institutional response. Analysts recommend caution, citing intense competition and potential profitability issues amid government plans to maintain a minimum public float.

items.title: Inside Chris Hohn’s Top Hedge Fund
items.summary: Plus, James Anderson sees end to tech boom, SpaceX IPO trading frenzy and the Old Vic’s all-female Glengarry Glen Ross

items.title: Eicher Motors surges 5% as record Q4 profit draws bullish street bets
items.summary: Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy with a revised target of ₹8,400, raised from ₹8,000, calling the Q4 print broadly in line

items.title: Crude oil futures fall on hopes of US-Iran deal
items.summary: Analysts expect the market to trade cautiously even as US says talks Iran with are progressing in a constructive manner

items.title: AI Rally Drives Best Run in Global Momentum Stocks on Record
items.summary: The AI boom is giving momentum investors their best return in decades, as the world’s hottest stocks power ahead despite worries over potentially slower growth due to the Iran war.

items.title: Crude oil prices fall 6% amid US-Iran peace deal hopes; MCX crude below ₹8,800 per barrel
items.summary: Brent crude futures declined 5.7% to $97.69 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate were at $90.85 a barrel, down 6%. Both contracts touched their lowest since May 7 earlier in the session.

items.title: India secures 2nd rank among EMs in April on oil-led export rebound
items.summary: Surging crude oil prices and disruptions around West Asia continued to test emerging economies in April, but India strengthened its position for the same reason.

items.title: BPCL, IOC to HPCL: OMC stocks surge up to 6% after Brent crude slips below $98, 4th hike in petrol & diesel prices
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) led the gainers with a 5.8% rise in its shares to ₹412.55 apiece. It was followed by Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL), whose stock added 4.44% to ₹308.70. Meanwhile, Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) jumped 3.90% to ₹144.95 on the BSE today.

items.title: Jardines signals pivot with $2.4bn Australian radiology deal
items.summary: Acquisition of I-MED is Hong Kong-based conglomerate’s first since strategic overhaul

items.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Dharmesh Shah of ICICI Sec suggests buying Tata Motors PV, BHEL shares on 25 May
items.summary: Domestic benchmark indices opened higher on May 25, buoyed by Brent crude prices dropping below $100 per barrel and optimism over a potential Iran peace deal. Nifty 50 rose 0.94% and BSE Sensex gained 1.02%. Analysts recommend buying Tata Motors and BHEL shares.

items.title: Rupee climbs 0.4% to 95.3425 on oil relief, RBI valuation comment
items.summary: The currency may now be undervalued following its recent slide versus the dollar, RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said

items.title: Australian FM Penny Wong to visit India for Quad FMM
items.summary: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong will visit India for a crucial Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting. She will also hold discussions with her Indian counterpart, Dr. S. Jaishankar, for the Australia-India Foreign Ministers' Framework Dialogue. The Quad partnership is vital for a peaceful Indo-Pacific. Ministers will discuss maritime security, critical minerals, and infrastructure development.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty surge over 1% as crude tumbles on US–Iran deal hopes; Eicher Motors leads gainers
items.summary: Sensex rises over 850 points in early trade; rupee recovers and auto, financial stocks lead gains

items.title: Gold offered at ₹450/g discount to Indian consumers after import duty hike
items.summary: The bullion trade is passing on the benefits of importing the yellow metal at lower duty to spur demand

items.title: Investors can no longer rely on a ‘policy put’
items.summary: The capacity of policymakers to respond to shocks is down across the board

items.title: It’s not just SpaceX: Big Tech is dominating bond markets too
items.summary: US tech giants are tapping markets as they race to build AI data centres

items.title: Can Exxon build the world’s biggest carbon capture business?
items.summary: Oil major bets billions on technology but backlash on US Gulf Coast threatens its ambitions

items.title: Fertiliser groups cut production as Iran war squeezes sulphur supplies
items.summary: Farmers in poorer countries risk lower yields as they reduce how much phosphate they apply to crops

items.title: The rupee may be undervalued, Sanjay Malhotra tells Mint
items.summary: A gauge of the rupee’s competitiveness against other currencies stood at 90.96 in April, showing the currency is likely undervalued against peers

items.title: Gold jumps as prospects of Iran deal temper inflation concerns
items.summary: Bullion rose as much as 1.6% to around $4,580 an ounce, erasing a moderate loss from last week

items.title: Who was Toshifumi Suzuki? All about the founder of Japan’s iconic Seven‑Eleven stores who died at 93
items.summary: Toshifumi Suzuki, the former chairman of Seven & i Holdings and founder of Seven-Eleven Japan, has died at 93. Credited with transforming Japan’s retail industry, Suzuki introduced the convenience store franchise model in 1973 and helped build Seven-Eleven into a global retail giant.

items.title: RBI ready to do 'whatever is required' to curb rupee volatility: Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is ready to act to stabilize the foreign exchange market. Governor Sanjay Malhotra indicated the rupee may be undervalued. The central bank has tools and reserves to manage volatility. Inflation remains the primary focus for monetary policy. India's economic fundamentals are strong despite global pressures.

items.title: Huawei plans 1.4nm chips by 2031 as China races to catch TSMC
items.summary: Huawei is working to reduce its five-year semiconductor production gap with TSMC by producing 1.4-nanometer chips by 2031.

items.title: Oil drops below $100 on Iran peace hopes, but fuel prices rise again
items.summary: Brent crude fell over 4% below $100 a barrel on hopes of an Iran-US peace deal, but Indian fuel retailers raised petrol and diesel prices for the fourth time in two weeks.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Trump keeps the world guessing with mixed messaging on Iran deal
items.summary: While he says that Washington will not rush into a deal, energy stockpiles risk hitting the red zone this summer.

items.title: How Saudi Arabia's spending spree reached the end of the line
items.summary: MBS's Vison 2030 project seemed the stuff of science fiction. Now reality has bitten

items.title: AI fever and off-exchange boom grip Japanese retail traders
items.summary: Average daily trading volume on Tokyo Stock Exchange’s prime market almost doubles in 12 months

items.title: RBI will do whatever it takes to curb undue speculation: Governor Malhotra
items.summary: Sanjay Malhotra speaks on RBI's priorities in inflation versus growth, the current account, forex market interventions, and the trajectory of  the rupee.

items.title: Flipkart bets games and AI can turn Bharat’s next internet users into shoppers
items.summary: Shopsy is being rebuilt around feeds, rewards and entertainment as Flipkart prioritizes digital habit formation over immediate purchases in India’s hyper-value e-commerce market.

items.title: India's solar export boom collapses as US tariffs bite
items.summary: All but one of India’s top listed solar module companies reported nil exports during the quarter ended 31 March. Market leader Waaree Energies Ltd, which had so far managed to protect its exports to the US, also reported fewer outbound shipments during the fourth quarter of FY26.

items.title: India's EV cell makers see first real shot at closing China price gap
items.summary: Lithium-ion cell makers are intensifying talks with the government to cut costs and build a local supplier base, as a 9% rise in imported Chinese cell prices offers a window to narrow the price gap. The push is urgent as EV cell imports hit $4.6 billion in FY26, over two-thirds of it from China.

items.title: 'I live in survival mode': The rise of the multi-job workforce
items.summary: More people are taking second jobs as rising costs and insecure work reshape how we earn a living.

items.title: Visa-backed PayMate ties unpaid employee dues to long-pending DigiAsia deal, funding
items.summary: PayMate, the Visa-backed B2B payments firm that once chased a ₹1,500-crore IPO, has been unable to pay full salaries to some staff for up to a year, linking repayment to the closure of a restructured deal with DigiAsia Corp.

items.title: AI promised cost savings, but Microsoft and Uber say it’s costing more than human workers
items.summary: Microsoft has cancelled most of its Claude Code licences and Uber burned through its 2026 AI budget in four months. New data reveals why enterprise AI costs are rising even as token prices fall.

items.title: Beer boom goes flat as breweries call last orders
items.summary: The UK's brewery scene is shrinking as pubs close, costs rise and drinking habits change.

items.title: RBI governor feels rupee may be undervalued after recent depreciation
items.summary: The rupee is not overvalued and may, in fact, be undervalued after its recent depreciation against the dollar, RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra says in an interview.

items.title: Top IT firms' H-1B visas slump 40%, TCS worst hit while Infosys gains
items.summary: H-1B visa approvals for India’s top IT services firms fell 40% this year to 11,041. As the Trump administration cracks down on work visas and green cards, IT firms are shifting towards more offshore work and local hiring in the US.

items.title: India’s Q4 GDP growth seen at 7.3% amid Iran war tensions
items.summary: India's economy finished fiscal year 2026 strongly. Growth in the March quarter was boosted by domestic demand, agriculture, and services. Economists predict 7.3% GDP growth for the quarter. Full-year growth is projected at 7.6%. Higher crude prices may impact the current fiscal year. Official data releases on June 5.

items.title: Morocco wants tourists to visit Western Sahara. Some say it's tightening its control
items.summary: The Moroccan government wants more Western holidaymakers to visit the territory it claims to own.

items.title: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
items.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

items.title: Space rockets, satellites, data centers and Grok: What's the right S&P sector index for SpaceX?
items.summary: What S&P Sector is SpaceX likely to be in once it launches on the public markets.

items.title: Trump not rushing Iran deal, whacks critics as 'losers'
items.summary: The president said the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports would remain in effect until "an agreement is reached, certified, and signed."

items.title: Delivery cost increased by about 19% in Asia Pacific: Report
items.summary: Delivery costs in India and the Asia Pacific region saw a significant rise of nearly 19 percent year-on-year. This increase occurred between March and May. Customers in India are now showing a preference for reliable delivery schedules rather than just speed. Many are willing to pay extra for convenient delivery options.

items.title: ECB Convenes Banks to Fix Flaws Exposed by AI Models, FT Says
items.summary: The European Central Bank plans to press lenders to accelerate efforts to secure their IT systems after organizing a meeting on cybersecurity risks with the latest artificial intelligence models, the Financial Times reported.

items.title: ECB summons banks to urge them to fix flaws exposed by latest AI models
items.summary: Supervisor to stress seriousness of risks to financial system at hastily arranged meeting

items.title: Financial reforms set to give £1.6bn boost to City of London
items.summary: Government assessment comes as Rachel Reeves champions district as driver of economic growth

items.title: Short sellers reap more than $2.3bn profit betting against gambling companies
items.summary: Online groups under pressure from jump in prediction markets’ popularity in US and steep tax rises in UK

items.title: Government allocates 8,606 tonnes of raw sugar exports to the US
items.summary: "The quantity of 8606 MTRV (metric tonnes raw value) raw cane sugar to be exported to USA under TRQ scheme from 01.10.2025 to 30.09.2026 has been notified," the DGFT has said in notification.

items.title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' is Disney's lowest-ever Star Wars film opening
items.summary: Disney's "The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied and estimated $82 million in domestic ticket sales through its first three days in theaters.

items.title: Marco Rubio meets NSA Doval, discusses defence, security and strategic tech cooperation including TRUST initiative
items.summary: US Secretary of State and National Security Advisor Marco Rubio met with Indian NSA Ajit Doval to bolster defense, security, and strategic technology cooperation, including the TRUST initiative. Discussions also covered regional and global developments, reaffirming the strong India-US Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership.

items.title: Barriers grow for international students seeking U.S. jobs: The 'American dream ... is collapsing'
items.summary: International graduates say a weak hiring market and changing immigration rules make it harder for them to achieve their American dream of working in the U.S.

items.title: I’ve worked with over 1,000 kids—the ones with the best people skills use these 6 phrases
items.summary: "Strong people skills are not the same as people-pleasing," says child life specialist and therapist Kelsey Mora, who's supported thousands of kids and families.

items.title: The Middle East war is testing the Gulf's ambitions to become an AI hub
items.summary: Attacks on data centers in the Middle East and persistently high energy prices have altered the calculus for operators of the facilities, analysts say.

items.title: Critical minerals, AI, nuclear ties take centre stage as Jaishankar, Rubio review India-US partnership
items.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar held extensive discussions with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, covering critical minerals, AI, and nuclear energy. The talks reviewed the full spectrum of bilateral ties, including trade, defense, and counter-terrorism, ahead of the Quad foreign ministers' meeting.

items.title: Centerbridge in talks to buy stake in $3bn real estate firm
items.summary: Potential investment in Merritt Properties comes amid a busy stretch for acquisitions in commercial property

items.title: India, US trade deal: Both countries looking at firming up pact soon
items.summary: India and the US are close to finalizing a trade agreement. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed significant progress. This deal aims to be beneficial and sustainable for both countries. It will advance mutual interests and boost cooperation in key sectors. A US trade delegation will visit India soon to further discussions on the proposed trade deal.

items.title: Iran war could add billions of dollars in interest payments to US debt
items.summary: Government borrowing costs have reached their highest levels since 2007 after three months of the conflict

items.title: Why IPO mania could signal top of the market
items.summary: A potential deluge of AI-related equity supply removes a source of upthrust for prices

items.title: 'Shameful' more spent on benefits than jobs for young people, says Milburn
items.summary: Reforms are needed of the welfare system to tackle the high numbers of young people not in work or education, says Alan Milburn.

items.title: As smartwatch fears fade, Ethos expands deeper into luxury watches
items.summary: Ethos says analogue watches are regaining relevance as aspiration-led purchases, prompting the retailer to expand stores, target younger buyers and push into luxury lifestyle categories despite margin pressure.

items.title: A $500 billion question hangs over Rubio’s India trade claim as original tariff bargain loses its footing
items.summary: The potential $500 billion investment by India in US goods is now being closely examined. According to findings from a trade advocacy group, the core underpinnings of the deal are showing signs of instability. This scrutiny follows a recent Supreme Court decision in the US that affects tariff structures, removing some exclusive advantages for specific nations.

items.title: US overtakes Mauritius to become India’s 2nd largest FDI source as inflows cross $11 billion mark
items.summary: The investment landscape is changing. The United States has now claimed the title of India's second-largest foreign investor, overtaking Mauritius. Equity infusions from America more than doubled to exceed $11 billion during the 2025-26 period. While Singapore holds the crown, sectors such as food processing, computer hardware, and shipping are experiencing exciting development.

items.title: Piyush Goyal to visit Canada next week with 150-member industry delegation; trade talks on agenda
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal will lead a large business delegation to Canada next week. The visit aims to speed up free trade agreement talks and boost investment. Discussions will focus on market access for Indian goods and securing critical minerals from Canada. Both nations target significant trade growth. Canadian companies in India are also set to increase.

items.title: India should now focus on FTA implementation, utilisation by exporters: Experts
items.summary: For India to leverage its Free Trade Agreements effectively, a paradigm shift in strategy is imperative. Experts caution that the disparity between secured market access and the realization of exports is widening.

items.title: City's living costs drive workers to food banks
items.summary: It is so expensive to live in Cambridge a charity says people with jobs need subsidised food.

items.title: The green strategic partnership: India and Norway chart a sustainable future
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s May 2026 visit to Norway — the first by an Indian Prime Minister in over 40 years — upgraded bilateral ties into a Green Strategic Partnership focused on technology, energy, defence, innovation and sustainability.

items.title: Govt in 'advanced stage of consultation' on industry's demand for cotton import duty removal
items.summary: The Indian government is actively considering removing the 11 percent customs duty on raw cotton imports. Ministries are in advanced talks to address cost pressures faced by the domestic textile industry. A supply-demand gap for cotton is creating challenges for spinning mills and manufacturers. A decision on the duty is expected soon.

items.title: OpenAI will pay ₹3.7 crore to worry about a problem that may not exist yet. The job? Stop AI from building itself
items.summary: OpenAI is hiring a safety researcher at up to   ₹3.7 crore ($445,000) to prepare for AI that can train itself. The job listing asks for someone ‘tasteful and strategic’ enough to reason about risks that do not yet exist.

items.title: Japan’s Akazawa Says He Spoke Briefly With China Commerce Minister
items.summary: Japanese Trade Minister Ryosei Akazawa and Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao had a brief chat on Friday, the first ministerial-level exchange of words between the two nations since a diplomatic standoff started in November.

items.title: 'Six eggs used to be £1' - why everyday essentials cost so much more now
items.summary: Six supermarket brand eggs cost £1 in 2022. How much are they now, why have they gone up, and is anyone profiteering?

items.title: No signs of industrial degrowth in India; readying plan to spur investments in some sectors: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal said the Centre is preparing several sector-specific investment promotion proposals announced in the budget. “We are continuously addressing challenges related to increasing self-reliance, particularly in areas where our supply chains are critically dependent on certain geographies,” he said.

items.title: No love in Tokyo for mangoes from India this season
items.summary: Japan has suspended fresh mango imports from India due to concerns over fumigation and disinfection deficiencies identified during a March inspection. This halt affects popular Indian varieties, with shipments certified after March 25, 2026, being rejected. The suspension will continue until India demonstrates improved operational standards.

items.title: Exports in high growth trajectory during May: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's exports are growing strongly in May, continuing April's pace. Foreign direct investment has reached an all-time high. The nation is seen as a preferred investment destination. The government is supporting farmers and introducing economic growth initiatives. A significant achievement is the upcoming semiconductor plant in Gujarat.
